title: PASSERINI MULTICOMPONENT REACTION
family_names: Passerini Multicomponent Reaction|Passerini reaction|Passerini three-component reaction
summary: Overview page for the Passerini multicomponent reaction describing isocyanide-based three-component coupling of a carboxylic acid, a carbonyl compound, and an isocyanide to furnish α-acyloxycarboxamides or related heterocyclic products.
notes: Key concepts: one-pot multicomponent assembly, broad aldehyde/ketone and acid scope, isocyanide insertion into activated carbonyl–carboxylate adducts, and variants using water or hydrazoic acid to access α-hydroxycarboxamides or tetrazoles.

title: PASSERINI MULTICOMPONENT REACTION
family_names: Passerini Multicomponent Reaction|Passerini reaction|Passerini three-component reaction
summary: Application page for the Passerini multicomponent reaction featuring Eurystatin A, nor-statine library members, and densely functionalized oxofuran carboxamides, highlighting rapid convergence in peptide-mimetic and heterocycle synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize mild convergent assembly of complex acyloxycarboxamides, use in combinatorial library generation, O→N acyl migration after deprotection, and downstream Knoevenagel condensation to furan scaffolds.

title: PATERNO-BÜCHI REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Paternò-Büchi reaction describing photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition of excited carbonyl compounds with alkenes to furnish oxetanes, often with predictable regioselectivity based on 1,4-biradical intermediates.
notes: Key concepts: UV-light excitation of aldehydes or ketones, singlet/triplet carbonyl pathways, alkene addition to form 1,4-biradicals, collapse to oxetanes, and stereochemical outcomes governed by substitution pattern and biradical conformation.

title: PATERNO-BÜCHI REACTION
summary: Application page for the Paternò-Büchi reaction featuring (+)-preussin, (±)-oxosilphiperfol-6-ene, (±)-euplotin A, and (±)-avenaciolide, showcasing intra- and intermolecular oxetane formation in natural-product synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize facially selective photocycloaddition, intramolecular fragmentation cascades, hetero Diels–Alder/Paternò-Büchi sequencing, and furan–aldehyde photochemical aldol surrogacy.

title: PAUSON-KHAND REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Pauson-Khand reaction describing cobalt-mediated formal [2+2+1] cycloaddition of an alkyne, an alkene, and carbon monoxide to generate substituted cyclopentenones.
notes: Key concepts: dicobalt-alkyne complexes, carbon monoxide insertion, alkene coordination, regioselective cyclization to cyclopentenones, and promoter-assisted variants using N-oxides, sulfides, light, or alternative metal carbonyl catalysts.

title: PAUSON-KHAND REACTION
summary: Application page for the Pauson-Khand reaction featuring (+)-taylorione, the CD quinane substructure of kalmanol, (+)-epoxydictymene, and 13-deoxyserratine, illustrating intra- and intermolecular cyclopentenone annulation in terpene and alkaloid synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize promoter-controlled cobalt decomplexation, intramolecular ring fusion, tandem Nicholas/Pauson-Khand strategies, and conformational control in enyne cyclizations.

title: PAYNE REARRANGEMENT
summary: Overview page for the Payne rearrangement describing base-catalyzed intramolecular epoxide migration of 2,3-epoxy alcohols to isomeric epoxides, plus aza- and thia-Payne variants that access aziridino and thiirano alcohols.
notes: Key concepts: deprotonation to an alkoxide, 3-exo-tet ring opening and reclosure with inversion at C2, solvent/base dependence, epoxide equilibration, and hetero-Payne variants promoted by base or Lewis acid.

title: PAYNE REARRANGEMENT
summary: Application page for the Payne rearrangement featuring epi-7-deoxypancratistatin, (−)-dysiherbaine, a sulfur-containing novel nucleoside, and (+)-merrilactone A, highlighting epoxide migration as a stereochemical relay in natural-product synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize aza-Payne trapping with electrophiles, benzylative capture of rearranged alkoxides, thia-Payne stereochemical relay to thiiranes, and acid-induced homo-Payne rearrangement to oxetane frameworks.

title: PERKIN REACTION
family_names: Perkin Reaction|Perkin condensation|Erlenmeyer-Plöchl azlactone synthesis|Oglialoro modification
summary: Overview page for the Perkin reaction describing condensation of aromatic aldehydes with acid anhydrides or N-acyl glycine derivatives under basic conditions to produce α,β-unsaturated carboxylic acids or azlactones.
notes: Key concepts: aromatic aldehyde preference, enolization of anhydrides or azlactones, β-alkoxide formation followed by elimination, mixed-anhydride variants, and utility in cinnamic-acid and α-amino-acid precursor synthesis.

title: PERKIN REACTION
family_names: Perkin Reaction|Perkin condensation|Erlenmeyer-Plöchl azlactone synthesis|Oglialoro modification
summary: Application page for the Perkin reaction featuring (Z)-combretastatin A-4, (S)-AIDA, ebalzotan (NAE-086), and 6-fluoro-meta-tyrosine, showing olefin-forming condensations in medicinal and natural-product synthesis.
notes: Examples emphasize stereocontrolled cinnamic-acid formation, subsequent decarboxylation or cyclization, azlactone chemistry for α-amino-acid analogs, and scale-up utility for bioactive scaffolds.
